answer.text: Preparedness to meet long-term goals such as retirement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Accumulated Savings-to-Income Ratio = Total Savings / Annual Income. It measures how well the individual has built savings relative to their income — a key indicator of long-term goal preparedness, especially retirement. **The Trap:** This is different from the monthly savings ratio. It's a stock measure (total accumulated savings) vs a flow measure. **Cross-Exam Context:** A suitable ratio in the early 40s is at least 3 times annual income, as stated in the X-A workbook. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a 40-year-old earning Rs. 12 lakh/year doesn't have at least Rs. 36 lakh in accumulated savings, retirement planning needs urgent attention.
